data_flow_overview.description: MoodScanner captures multimodal mood data → moodProcessor analyzes → storage.saveMood persists → getMoodHistory loads

1_data_structure_mismatches.title: Score Property Name Inconsistency
1_data_structure_mismatches.description: moodProcessor.analyzeMultiModal() returns 'score' as 'stabilityScore' but other code expects 'score'

location_saved.context: analysis object contains: score (stabilityScore from moodProcessor)

location_processor.context: Returns object with 'score' property set to stabilityScore variable

1_data_structure_mismatches.impact: Historical mood data accessed but score property might be undefined, causing invalid trend analysis
1_data_structure_mismatches.root_cause: Variable naming confusion: 'stabilityScore' computed internally but exported as 'score'

1_data_structure_mismatches.title: Saved Data Structure Exceeds Expected Format
1_data_structure_mismatches.description: saveMood() adds extra properties (id, timestamp) to data that already has multimodalData and metrics
1_data_structure_mismatches.impact: Data structure is deeply nested and complex; loading code may not handle all nested properties correctly

1_data_structure_mismatches.title: Expected Load Structure Not Fully Defined
1_data_structure_mismatches.description: getMoodHistory() returns raw array but consumers expect specific structure with .score property

location.code: async getMoodHistory() { return await this.getItem('mood_history', { defaultValue: [] }); }

1_data_structure_mismatches.impact: If score property missing, defaults to 50, masking data corruption

2_timestamp_duplication.title: Dual Timestamp Creation
2_timestamp_duplication.description: Timestamp created twice: once in moodProcessor, once in storage.saveMood()

timestamp_3.where: saveMood() adds ANOTHER timestamp when persisting
timestamp_3.created_at: When data is written to storage (milliseconds later)

2_timestamp_duplication.impact: Timestamps differ by milliseconds; unclear which one represents actual mood analysis time vs persistence time
2_timestamp_duplication.recommendation: Use single timestamp from moodProcessor, not storage

2_timestamp_duplication.title: Timestamp Skew Due to Async Delay
2_timestamp_duplication.description: Time gap between moodProcessor timestamp creation and storage.saveMood() persistence
2_timestamp_duplication.gap: Depends on processMoodAnalysis() execution time, typically 500-2000ms
2_timestamp_duplication.issue: Historical trend analysis uses wrong timestamp, causing circadian/temporal calculations to be off

3_async_initialization_issues.title: Storage Initialization Not Awaited
3_async_initialization_issues.description: AdvancedStorage.init() is async but called in constructor without await

init_function.does: Run migrations, start TTL cleanup, set initialized=true
init_function.problem: May not be complete when first getItem/setItem called

3_async_initialization_issues.impact: Storage operations may execute before migrations complete, before cache TTL interval starts

timing_race.created: storage singleton instantiated in index.js during Redux store setup
timing_race.risk: Depends on how long Redux setup takes (PersistGate probably waits, but unclear)

3_async_initialization_issues.title: moodProcessor Uses localStorage in React Native
3_async_initialization_issues.description: moodProcessor._loadUserModel() and _saveUserModel() use browser localStorage, which doesn't exist in React Native
3_async_initialization_issues.impact: User model (weights, baselines, kalman state) NEVER persists in actual app; only stored in memory for session
3_async_initialization_issues.consequence: Personalization is reset on app restart; learning doesn't accumulate
3_async_initialization_issues.fix_required: Replace localStorage with AsyncStorage or use centralized storage utility

3_async_initialization_issues.title: processMoodAnalysis Lacks Null Checks Before Async Calls
3_async_initialization_issues.description: processMoodAnalysis() doesn't verify storage or moodProcessor are initialized
3_async_initialization_issues.risk: If storage initialization incomplete, getMoodHistory() might access uninitialized state

4_property_name_mismatches.title: Stabilization Score Naming Confusion
4_property_name_mismatches.description: Internal variable stabilityScore becomes property score on export
4_property_name_mismatches.potential_confusion: Code readers might think 'score' is distinct from 'stabilityScore'

4_property_name_mismatches.title: Multi-Modal Data Properties Vary in Naming
4_property_name_mismatches.description: Different emotion objects use different property names

inconsistencies.returns: { happy, sad, angry, fearful, surprised, neutral }
inconsistencies.normalizes_from: sentimentAnalyzer output which uses { joy, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, disgust, neutral }

inconsistencies.note: Different structure than raw emotion objects

inconsistencies.vs_multimodal_data: { textEmotions, facialEmotions, voiceEmotions } each have emotion properties

5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.title: Redux dataSlice Not Connected to Mood Data Flow
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.description: Mood data is saved directly to AsyncStorage via storage.saveMood(), not through Redux

redux_location.has: createAsyncThunk for fetchItems, addItem, updateItem, deleteItem

5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.impact: Mood data bypasses Redux entirely; not available in Redux state for other components
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.consequence: Dashboard can't access mood history from Redux; must call storage.getMoodHistory() directly
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.consistency_issue: Some data (user) in Redux, mood data direct to AsyncStorage; two different patterns

5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.title: No Redux Action for Mood Analysis Results
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.description: processMoodAnalysis completes but result never dispatched to Redux
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.should_dispatch: setResult(analysis) sets local state only; should also dispatch Redux action
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.affected_components: Any component needing current mood must use local state prop drilling or call storage directly

5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.title: Offline Queue Configuration Doesn't Cover Mood Saves
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.description: offlineSlice handles offline actions but mood saves aren't wrapped in offline queue
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.problem: If network down, data saved to AsyncStorage but not queued for sync; lost on next app restart if cache cleared
5_redux_vs_storage_conflicts.expected_pattern: Should dispatch offlineSlice.enqueueAction() with mood save action

6_import_export_inconsistencies.title: moodProcessor Export Inconsistency
6_import_export_inconsistencies.description: Default export is instance, named export is class; inconsistent with how it's used

location.code:
export const moodProcessor = new MoodProcessor();
export { MoodProcessor };

6_import_export_inconsistencies.potential_issue: If multiple instances needed (e.g., per-user), must import MoodProcessor class, not default

6_import_export_inconsistencies.title: Storage Import Pattern Varies Slightly
6_import_export_inconsistencies.description: storage imported as default, but exports both AdvancedStorage class and instance
6_import_export_inconsistencies.consistency: Fine - allows both `import storage` and `import { AdvancedStorage }`

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.title: Synchronous Redux Persist Initialization Blocks Metro
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.description: Redux persistor created and PersistGate wraps app, but may block bundle if async operations not handled
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.issue: If persistConfig migration takes too long, metro bundler waits; no timeout

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.title: localStorage Type Check in moodProcessor Constructor
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.description: Code checks `typeof localStorage !== 'undefined'` but in React Native, this check might cause bundler issues if polyfills interfere
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.bundler_concern: If bundler tries to resolve localStorage polyfill and hits circular dependency or slow module, Metro hangs

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.title: Storage Singleton Init() Called Without Await During Import
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.description: storage.js invokes this.init() in constructor synchronously, starts async operations

location.issue: storageInstance created and init() called immediately, but init() is async and starts intervals

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.bundler_hang_mechanism: If async init() has side effects that block module resolution, metro waits

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.title: Possible Circular Import Between Slices and Utils
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.description: offlineSlice imports persistReducer, index.js imports offlineSlice; if utils/storage imported by offlineSlice, circular
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.risk: Metro bundler might trace circular dependencies and hang trying to resolve module order

7_bundler_hang_root_causes.title: Uncontrolled RetryQueue Processing
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.description: storage.processRetryQueue() uses setTimeout but doesn't have explicit timeout/cancellation for bundler
7_bundler_hang_root_causes.risk: If retryQueue has many failed operations at startup, processRetryQueue infinite loops during bundling

8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.title: No Validation Before Saving Mood Data
8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.description: saveMood() accepts any object without schema validation

location.code: async saveMood(moodData) { const history = await this.getItem(...); history.push({ ...moodData, ... });

8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.risk: Corrupted or partial analysis object could be saved; no way to detect on load

8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.title: History Array Unbounded Until Truncation
8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.description: getMoodHistory retrieves full array before slicing to 100 items on save
8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.issue: If history corrupted or grows beyond 100, entire array loaded then sliced; memory inefficient

8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.title: Checksum Validation Doesn't Catch Schema Changes
8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.description: Checksum validates data integrity but not schema correctness

location.code: const expectedChecksum = simpleHash(payload.data); if (payload.checksum !== expectedChecksum) { return defaultValue; }

8_data_consistency_and_corruption_risks.scenario: Old mood data with missing properties (e.g., multimodalData) passes checksum but fails on load

options.previousMoods: Array (optional, default []) - CRITICAL: must have .score property

processing_steps: Compute historical trend boost using previousMoods.score
Map to mood profile and compute stabilityScore

output_structure.recommendation: object { chakra, flower, book, affirmations[], todos[] }
output_structure.metrics: object { valence, arousal, biometricStability, avgResponseTime, personalBias }
output_structure.details: object { circadian, physiological, cognitive, behavioral, historical, kalmanGain }

adds_multimodal_data.note: Added to analysis object before saving

adds.timestamp: new Date().toISOString() DUPLICATE OF PROCESSOR TIMESTAMP

saves_to_mood_history.structure: Array of { all previous props + id + timestamp }

stage_5_storage_getMoodHistory.critical_requirement: Each entry MUST have .score property (line 265 of moodProcessor)

step_1.what: User initiates mood scan, MoodScanner state transitions to 'processing'

step_3.what: Get previous mood history from storage
step_3.risk: First async operation; getMoodHistory might fail if storage not initialized

step_4.what: deserializePayload extracts mood_history from encrypted/compressed storage

step_5.note: previousMoods used for trend analysis; CRITICAL: must have .score property
step_5.risk: If score property missing, trend analysis defaults to 50, masking cache corruption

step_6.what: Processor computes stabilityScore, creates timestamp NOW
step_6.note: This timestamp represents START of analysis, not when data saved

step_7.adds: textEmotions, facialEmotions, voiceEmotions, fusedEmotions, dominantEmotion, etc.

step_9.risk: If history large (100 items), deserialization decompression takes time

step_10.what: saveMood() adds ANOTHER timestamp and id to analysis
step_10.code: history.push({ ...moodData, id: Date.now().toString(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() })
step_10.timestamp_delta: Timestamp now differs from processor timestamp by ~600-700ms

step_11.risk: If compression/encryption enabled, further delays possible

step_13.what: setResult(analysis) updates local state, UI transitions to 'result' stage
step_13.displayed_to_user: analysis object with timestamps and multimodalData

critical_bugs_summary.title: localStorage in React Native
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: Code checks localStorage availability, finds none, silently fails to persist user model
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: Personalization never accumulates; every app start resets to default weights
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 2 hours - Replace localStorage with AsyncStorage in moodProcessor

critical_bugs_summary.title: Dual Timestamps
critical_bugs_summary.affects: Temporal analysis, trend calculations, circadian rhythm scoring
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: Mood has timestamp from processor (when analyzed) and from storage (when saved)
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: 600-700ms difference causes circadian calculations to be off by actual time-of-day
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 1 hour - Use processor timestamp only, remove storage timestamp

critical_bugs_summary.title: Storage Initialization Race Condition
critical_bugs_summary.affects: All storage operations if migrations haven't run
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: storage.init() is async but called without await in constructor
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: If app calls getMoodHistory() before init() completes, migrations skipped
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 2 hours - Wrap storage usage in await initialization, or use lazy init

critical_bugs_summary.title: Metadata Bloat in Saved Data
critical_bugs_summary.affects: Storage size, load time as history grows
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: Each mood entry saved with full multimodalData, metrics, details, recommendation objects
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: 100 mood entries could exceed 1-2MB; compression helps but adds CPU overhead
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 4 hours - Separate mood summary from detail data; store details separately

critical_bugs_summary.title: No Redux Integration for Mood Data
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: Mood data saved directly to AsyncStorage, bypasses Redux
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: Offline queue doesn't cover mood saves; other components can't access via Redux
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 6 hours - Create mood-related Redux slices, dispatch after save

critical_bugs_summary.title: No Data Validation Before Save
critical_bugs_summary.affects: Data integrity, ability to detect corruption
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: saveMood() accepts any object without schema check
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: Corrupted analysis could be saved; no way to detect on load except checksum
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 3 hours - Add JSON schema validation for mood entries

critical_bugs_summary.title: Missing .score Property in Historical Array
critical_bugs_summary.behavior: If historical mood loaded without .score property, code defaults to 50
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: Trend boost calculation incorrect; mood appears stable when it's not
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 1 hour - Add defensive check in analyzeMultiModal(), log mismatch

critical_bugs_summary.title: Bundler Hang - Multiple Possible Causes
potential_causes: localStorage type check in moodProcessor constructor
storage.init() async operations starting during module load
critical_bugs_summary.consequence: Bundler hangs for 30+ seconds; developer workflow blocked
critical_bugs_summary.fix_effort: 8 hours - Debug with Metro logs, isolate blocking module, refactor initialization

P0_immediate: Remove localStorage usage from moodProcessor.js; use AsyncStorage
Add await/initialization check before storage methods called in MoodScanner
Remove storage-added timestamp; use processor timestamp only
Add defensive console.log/logger.warn when score property missing from historical moods
P1_urgent: Create Redux mood slice; dispatch mood save action to queue instead of direct storage
Add JSON schema validation to saveMood()
Separate mood summary from detailed metrics; lazy-load on demand
Add timeout to storage initialization; fail fast if stuck
P2_important: Benchmark storage load time with 100 mood entries; optimize if > 500ms
Create unit tests for moodProcessor with mock historical data
Add error handling in processMoodAnalysis for storage/processor failures
Profile Metro bundler startup; identify and isolate slow modules
P3_nice_to_have: Create middleware to sync mood data to cloud periodically
Implement mood data deduplication (if duplicate within 1 hour)

utils/moodProcessor.js.dependencies: NONE - no imports from other modules except constants/logger
issues: Uses conditional localStorage check (React Native incompatible)
No AsyncStorage import for React Native
Async init() not awaited in constructor
Uses setTimeout in retry logic without timeout
No connection to Redux offline queue
Potential circular import if slices import utils/storage
PersistGate loading state might not timeout

slices/offlineSlice.js.problem: React Native doesn't support IndexedDB; idb will fail
slices/offlineSlice.js.workaround: Code checks window.indexedDB existence; silently fails
slices/offlineSlice.js.consequence: Large offline queue won't persist across app restart

unit_tests: mapTextEmotionsToFusion() edge cases (null input, zero emotions)
integration_tests: Full mood scan flow: capture → analyze → save → load
Historical trend calculation using retrieved mood history
Offline mode: mood save attempted with no network
App restart: verify mood history persists and loads
Timestamp consistency: verify processor and storage timestamps
performance_tests: Load time for getMoodHistory() with 100 entries
Storage space used for 100 mood entries (expected ~1-2MB)
Metro bundler startup time before/after optimizations
